north_star_verdict.headline: New owner Claire Hudson is restarting this £500k NHS BSA deal through partner EY with a planned onsite, but the buyer has been silent since 28 Jan (five months), no economic buyer is mapped, and no business case is quantified, so the November close depends entirely on the reset landing.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 23 Jan 2026, last verified buyer touch 28 Jan 2026 (~159 days ago), 147 days to close 30 Nov 2026. Stage Qualified since 30 Jan 2026 (~5 months, no progression). Ownership moved Rozalyn Orme to Claire Hudson on 13 May 2026 and Next_Step was refreshed 16 Jun 2026, so rep/partner motion is recent even though buyer-side engagement is not. No submission-date milestones recorded. The onsite on 'the 22nd' with EY is rep-and-partner planning, not a confirmed buyer meeting, so treat it as outreach awaiting the buyer's reply.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case is documented anywhere for this deal. Next_Step (refreshed by Claire Hudson on 16 Jun 2026) references a 'KPI and ROI/ business case jointly built' with champion Darren Curry and partner EY, but names no cost-savings target, cycle-time gain, error-reduction, or headcount impact. The SF Description centres on NHS payments and the incumbent 'Arriba' rather than a measured pain. With no Avoma calls and no Customer_Business_Problem__c or Value_to_Customer__c content, the deal has no defensible value anchor, so co-authoring the quantified case with Darren and EY is the immediate move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unidentified. Next_Step names 'intro to execs' as an action still owed by champion Darren Curry, which tells us budget authority sits ABOVE Darren (Chief Digital, Data and Technology Officer) but does not name that person. No OpportunityContactRole carries an Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role, and no X10a_Sponsor__c / Who_will_approve_budget__c value is populated. For a £500k public-sector NHS spend the budget owner is almost certainly a Director or C-suite finance/transformation lead requiring internal business-case and Treasury-style approval. The 22 Jun EY onsite is framed as 'executive stakeholder alignment', so its whole purpose should be to surface and engage that budget owner; capturing who they are is the gating move.
sources: SF Next_Step__c (intro to execs requested, not completed)

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are not yet formalised. Next_Step positions the work as 'assisting prospect to get budget and business case/ shaping RFP', which explicitly places the deal PRE-requirements, before any RFP or BRD exists. No Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, or AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c content is present, and the 13-module scope (iSource, ML iAnalyze, iManage, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, AppXtend, Agentic AI, ANA) is listed but tied to no use cases or weighted selection factors. This is actually the opportunity: because criteria are unwritten, Zycus and EY can co-shape the RFP toward Zycus's differentiators (Merlin/agentic sourcing) before competitors are invited, which is the stated strategy.

decision_process.narrative: The process is early and partner-led. Next_Step describes Zycus positioning alongside EY to help NHS BSA secure internal budget and shape the RFP before a formal evaluation begins, with an onsite on 'the 22nd' for executive stakeholder alignment. EY's role as co-enabler suggests a consulting-influenced procurement where the business case must clear internal funding before tender. No What_is_the_decision_process__c or Mandate__c content, and no timeline for budget approval, RFP release, shortlist, or award is documented. For an NHS technology buy of this size expect an internal business case, funding approval, and a compliant public tender, but none of that is confirmed on the record, so the deal is effectively waiting on the budget/RFP kickoff the EY onsite is meant to unlock.
sources: SF Next_Step__c (budget + RFP shaping, EY onsite)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in view, which is normal for a Qualified pre-RFP deal. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal-review, or InfoSec/security-assurance step is documented, and there is no Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function. As an NHS public-sector purchase, eventual contracting will likely run through a public procurement framework with NHS security assurance (for example DSPT / Cyber Essentials), but that gate is premature at this stage. Partner EY may run procurement advisory, though its exact role (advisory, implementation, resale) is not defined on the record. Visibility will only come once budget is approved and the deal enters formal evaluation.

identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain is not articulated on the record. The SF Description centres on NHS payments and the incumbent 'Arriba', implying dissatisfaction with the current procurement/payments setup, but no named pain, pain owner, or compelling event is captured, and no Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c or Compelling_Event__c value exists. Next_Step shows Darren Curry building an internal case, which implies a recognised inefficiency worth a £500k investment, yet the underlying pain narrative is inferred, not stated. With zero Avoma calls there is no buyer quote to anchor it, so reconstructing the pain with Darren at the EY onsite is the priority before any RFP is shaped.

champion.narrative: Darren Curry (Chief Digital, Data and Technology Officer, OpportunityContactRole = Influencer, darren.curry@nhsbsa.nhs.uk) is the named and only mapped contact. Next_Step (refreshed 16 Jun 2026) describes him as a champion 'established and tested', able to influence the deal, co-enable the budget request on KPI/ROI, and provide internal info, exec introductions, and project coordination. That is real advocacy signal, but it comes entirely from the rep's field note, not from any buyer call, and no direct contact with Darren has been logged since 28 Jan 2026. His access to the economic buyer is asserted (he was asked for the exec intro) but not yet demonstrated. Net: a developing champion on paper who has gone quiet for five months and must be reactivated at the 22 Jun onsite, with the exec intro as the proof of his influence.

competition.narrative: The only competitor named in evidence is the incumbent 'Arriba', referenced in the SF Description as the current system NHS BSA runs for its payments/procurement, so Zycus is positioned as a displacement play against that incumbent. No active rival has been named in any field, task, or call, and Next_Step frames the deal as pre-RFP ('shaping RFP'), meaning a competitive shortlist has not yet formed. The strategic read is favourable-but-fragile: by co-shaping the RFP with EY before it is published, Zycus can pre-empt rivals, but once NHS BSA runs a compliant public tender other suite vendors will be invited, so speed to a shaped RFP is the wedge. No speculative rivals are asserted because none appear in the evidence.

deal_movement.summary: Deal is buyer-dark since 28 Jan 2026 but has fresh rep/partner motion: ownership moved to Claire Hudson on 13 May 2026 and she refreshed the Next_Step on 16 Jun 2026 describing an EY-led onsite for executive alignment. No stage progression from Qualified and no buyer-confirmed activity yet.

competitive_position.summary: Single-vendor, pre-RFP positioning against the incumbent 'Arriba' (SF Description), which NHS BSA currently runs for payments/procurement. No active rival has been named in any field or activity and no shortlist has formed, so the strongest current dynamic is the incumbent status quo, not a live bake-off. The win path is to co-shape the RFP with partner EY before it is published; once a public tender opens, suite competitors will be invited, so shaping speed is the wedge.
